choice: `.canon/` at repo root — new top-level directory
rationale: Meta-system deserves top-level visibility, not buried under .planning/ or docs/

choice: Registry JSONL (`.canon/ecosystems.jsonl`) + per-ecosystem detail files
rationale: JSONL = canonical/AI-consumed, MD = human views generated from JSONL. Core tenet of the system moving forward.

choice: Hybrid: completeness tier checklist drives computed level (L0-L5)
rationale: Makes 'what's missing' obvious and progress measurable. Concrete capabilities compute to a level.

choice: Per-ecosystem files are source of truth + generated matrix view
rationale: Follows JSONL-first → generated-views pattern. Each ecosystem knows its own subsystems, matrix is computed.

choice: Global defaults + per-ecosystem cascade/override (CSS-like)
rationale: Avoids 70+ hardcoded values while letting ecosystems specialize where needed.

choice: Dependency graph (CANON first) with maturity as tiebreaker (highest first)
rationale: Build momentum: early wins validate the framework before tackling hard stuff. CANON → near-compliant → major overhauls.

choice: Full ecosystem catalog + maturity assessments + sequenced plan + CANON spec
rationale: We have the research (500+ components). Comprehensive output avoids another discovery pass.

choice: Approved scale: L0=Nonexistent, L1=Identified, L2=Structured, L3=Monitored, L4=Enforced, L5=Canonized
rationale: Clear progression. L5 = all applicable subsystems canonized (not all 16 if some don't apply to ecosystem).

sub_decisions.l5_for_everything: L5 is the north star, not the exit criteria. Most ecosystems target L3-L4 baseline. L5 means all *applicable* subsystems canonized — per-ecosystem required-vs-optional mapping.
sub_decisions.plan_structure: Plan-as-you-go with sequenced placeholders. This deep plan produces catalog + sequence. Each ecosystem gets its own deep-plan when its turn comes. Pre-planning all 13 now would produce stale plans by ecosystem #8.

choice: 16-item checklist (original 13 + 3 gap-fills: inter-ecosystem contracts, rollback/recovery, deprecation policy)
rationale: User liked original 13, accepted 3 additions to close gaps.
checklist: 1. Zod schemas — for all data structures
2. JSONL storage — canonical data in JSONL format
3. Generated views — MD/JSON views computed from JSONL
4. Health monitoring — at least one health checker with metrics
5. Enforcement manifest — rules defining what's blocking vs warning
6. Testing — unit + integration tests for ecosystem scripts
8. State persistence — survives compaction / session boundaries
10. Naming compliance — follows CANON naming conventions
11. Configuration — uses central config (not hardcoded values)
12. Lifecycle hooks — creation, update, archival, deletion defined
14. Inter-ecosystem contracts — interfaces between dependent ecosystems
15. Rollback/recovery — revert to prior state after bad migration
16. Deprecation policy — how old schemas/APIs/patterns are sunset

choice: Pattern-level: CANON defines interface + reference pattern (PR Review v2 as exemplar)
rationale: Templates too rigid, interface-only too ambiguous. Reference patterns formalize what already exists.

choice: Primary ownership + consumer references (no superseding ecosystem)
rationale: Primary = maintenance responsibility, not authority hierarchy. Shared components have one owner, many consumers. CANON defines rules but doesn't own other ecosystems' components.

decision: CANON spec scope in this plan
choice: Working draft (structure + maturity model + subsystem interfaces + registry schema) with room for growth built in everywhere
rationale: Enough to start building CANON. Ecosystem assessments separate. Room for growth is a core tenet — nothing ships as a dead end.

sub_decisions.upgradeability: Core tenet: nothing not-upgradeable. Ease the upgrade process. Everything must have room for growth. Things change all the time.

choice: Semver (start at 0.1.0, promote to 1.0.0 after checkpoint #1 — 4 ecosystem validations)
rationale: CANON is infrastructure others depend on. Breaking changes need explicit migration guidance.
amendment_note: Original said '2-3 ecosystem validations' which conflicted with D76's checkpoint #21 timeline. Resolved: 1.0.0 after checkpoint #1 (CANON, Skills, Hooks, PR Review — positions 1-4 in D67). D76 checkpoints 2-4 use 1.x minor versions.

choice: 4-tier: error (blocking), warning (non-blocking + logged), info (displayed only), silent (suppressed)
rationale: Granular enough without being complex. Silent tier handles known-accepted items.

choice: Tiered: pre-commit (fast checks), pre-push (full validation), PR (comprehensive)
rationale: Fast feedback for quick checks, thorough validation before sharing. Each gate has appropriate scope.

choice: Files + exports + JSONL fields (3-layer naming standard)
rationale: Covers the surfaces that matter most. Full taxonomy is overkill, files-only misses the data layer.

choice: Current interface + trend data + recommendations (enhanced)
rationale: Trend enables progress tracking over time. Recommendations make health checks actionable, not just diagnostic.
amendment_4T: Health checker implementations MUST be Node.js (T7 platform-agnostic). Added during tenets retroactive review.

choice: Hybrid: auto-generate on data change + on-demand for full rebuild
rationale: Stale dashboards are useless, but full rebuild on every change is wasteful. Incremental auto + manual full.

choice: Pilot + rollout: validate with 2-3 near-compliant ecosystems, then roll out to rest
rationale: Validates CANON itself before forcing it on complex ecosystems. Early pilots surface spec issues cheaply.

choice: Both: per-ecosystem declares own deps + central registry aggregates all
rationale: Per-ecosystem is source of truth (consistent with Decision #4). Central view is generated/computed for cross-cutting analysis.

choice: Hybrid: CANON infrastructure in categorized dirs + per-ecosystem dirs for assessment/detail data
rationale: Best of both worlds. CANON's own config/schemas/reports organized by function. Each ecosystem's data co-located in its own dir.

structure..canon/tenets.jsonl: Core tenets (first CANON artifact — CANON defines itself first)

choice: Extensible core: required core fields + optional fields with defaults. Schema validates required, accepts extra.
rationale: Registry line is summary/index. Detail files have full picture. Room for growth — new fields without breaking existing data.

choice: Per-ecosystem files: `.canon/ecosystems/{id}/assessment.jsonl` — co-located with ecosystem data
rationale: Keeps ecosystem data co-located (consistent with Decision #4). Each ecosystem owns its assessment. Matrix/summary views generated.

choice: Zod as source of truth → JSON Schema auto-generated for external tooling. JSONL tracks implementation status.
rationale: Follows established pattern: source of truth (Zod) → generated views (JSON Schema). Plus JSONL tracks adoption per ecosystem.

choice: JSON summary envelope (score, trend, metadata) + JSONL findings stream (one finding per line)
rationale: Summary is single object for dashboards. Individual findings are JSONL (appendable, processable). Best of both.

choice: Single JSONL file per ecosystem, each rule has `tier` + `severity` fields
rationale: Single file easier to maintain/query. Tier (pre-commit/pre-push/PR) + severity (error/warning/info/silent from #14) per rule. Tools filter by tier at runtime.

choice: Standardized script interface: each view is a script with contract (reads JSONL → outputs MD). Standard CLI interface (--input, --output).
rationale: Standardized interface, not implementation. Scripts can be simple or complex — the contract is what's standardized. Follows Decision #10 pattern-level standardization.
amendment_4T: Scripts MUST be Node.js (T7 platform-agnostic) and idempotent — same input always produces same output with no side effects (T12). Added during tenets retroactive review.

decision: Core tenets discovery phase — inserted before Batch 5
choice: Pause schema batches. Discover, formalize, and lock all core tenets BEFORE continuing with ecosystem assessments.
rationale: Core tenets guide everything downstream. Locking schemas before fully articulating the principles that shape them is backwards. Two tenets (automation over discipline, no forgettable processes) had already fallen through the cracks, proving the need.

decision: Tenet candidate disposition — promote, merge, demote
choice: Promote 6 candidates to formal tenets, merge 2 into existing tenets, demote 2 to implementation patterns.
rationale: Tenets are 'why' principles that guide decisions. Patterns are 'how' implementations. Cascade/override (D#5) and progressive disclosure are patterns, not principles.

merged.nothing_gets_lost: Merged into renamed tenet 'capture_everything_surface_what_matters' (combines visibility + institutional memory)
merged.discoveries_feed_forward: Merged as sub-principle of 'plan_as_you_go' (knowledge compounds, learnings from ecosystem #3 inform #4)

demoted.cascade_override: Implementation pattern (Decision #5), not a guiding principle. Referenced by tenets but isn't one.
demoted.progressive_disclosure: UX/design guideline for tooling. L0-L5 and tiered gates implement it, but it's not why they exist.

choice: Add platform_agnostic_by_default, idempotent_operations, fail_loud_fail_early. Formalize automation_over_discipline (was discussed but never recorded).
rationale: Platform-agnostic: dual-environment (Linux sandbox + Windows CLI) is a real constraint with existing breakage. Idempotency: prevents data corruption from re-runs (MASTER_DEBT race condition is a violation). Fail loud/early: complements visibility — errors must scream, not whisper. Automation over discipline: if it relies on human memory, it WILL fail.

dual_environment_impact.implications: All CANON scripts Node.js (not bash). Forward-slash paths. LF line endings. No symlinks. No platform-specific env vars without cross-platform fallback.
existing_breakage: 3 bash-only scripts have no Windows equivalent

choice: Option A: Flat list with 4 categories (Foundation, Design, Operations, Process). No parent-child hierarchy.
rationale: As tenets grow, a flat list becomes a wall. Categories make it scannable. No hierarchy avoids 'is X a child of Y?' debates. Easy to add new tenets to a category.
amendment_note: Removed hardcoded '16 tenets' per D90 (no hardcoded growing counts).

choice: `.canon/tenets.jsonl` (source of truth) + `.canon/tenets.md` (generated view). First artifact CANON produces about itself.
rationale: CANON defines itself first. Tenets depend on nothing else. Dog-foods JSONL-first pattern. Each line: {id, category, name, statement, evidence[], added, version}.

schema.name: snake_case identifier
schema.evidence: Array of decision IDs that informed/embody this tenet

amendment_note: Schema id changed from hardcoded T1-T16 range to T{n} auto-incrementing pattern. Tenet count will grow over time — hardcoding a range guarantees staleness.

decision: PR Review: Current and target maturity
choice: Current L4 (Enforced) → Target L5 (Canonized)
rationale: Reference implementation. 10/16 present, 4 partial, 2 absent. First ecosystem to reach L5 — validates CANON framework itself before applying to others.
assessment_summary: Strongest ecosystem. 56 test files, 10 health checkers, multi-mechanism enforcement. Gaps are post-v2 items (rollback, deprecation policy) and formalization (naming validation pending CANON, contracts pending format definition).

choice: Current L2 (Structured) → Target L5 (Canonized)
rationale: USER OVERRIDE: TDMS feeds into everything, MASTER_DEBT has thousands of entries, too critical to leave below L5. Acknowledged as massive effort across 37 scripts.
effort: XL (L2→L5 across 37 scripts with thousands of data entries)
staging: Staged: L2→L3 (Zod+monitoring) → L4 (enforcement) → L5 (canonized). Each stage is L effort.
assessment_summary: 3/16 present, 8 partial, 5 absent. No Zod schemas, no enforcement manifest, no versioning. 37 scripts with inconsistent patterns. Massive scope but critical system.

choice: Current L1 (Identified) → Target L3 (Monitored)
rationale: Cross-cutting infrastructure — every ecosystem depends on session state being reliable. Schemas + JSONL + monitoring gives reliability layer. Full enforcement (L4) too aggressive for infrastructure that needs graceful degradation.
assessment_summary: 2/16 present, 8 partial, 6 absent. State persistence works, lifecycle defined, but nothing else formalized. Runs on convention.

choice: Current L3 (Monitored) → Target L4 (Enforced)
rationale: Infrastructure that other ecosystems depend on. Strong operational automation, good monitoring. Delta is mainly Zod schemas + formalized contracts + versioning.
assessment_summary: 5/16 present, 6 partial, 5 absent. Strong enforcement (IS the enforcement system for the repo), good monitoring via ecosystem audit. Gaps in formalization.

choice: Current L1 (Identified) → Target L3 (Monitored)
rationale: USER OVERRIDE from L2→L3 staged to direct L3. Large undertaking across 65 skills. Skill-audit skill exists and was recently used on skill-creator and deep-plan with great results — that process should be leveraged during ecosystem standardization.
effort: L (65 skills, each needs ecosystem audit + standardization)
staging: Direct (L1→L3). Skill-audit skill drives per-skill assessment.
assessment_summary: 0/16 present, 8 partial, 8 absent. 65 skills, structurally validated but zero data tracking, zero formalized contracts, zero lifecycle management.
user_note: Skill-audit skill (recently created) follows processes enacted in refined skill-creator and deep-plan skills. All skills will need ecosystem auditing.

choice: Current L2 (Structured) → Target L4 (Enforced)
rationale: Mid-session alerting is a critical operational layer. Has 36 alert categories, scoring, benchmarks, trend tracking. Needs Zod schemas, enforcement manifest, versioning, and inter-ecosystem contracts formalized.
staging: Staged: L2→L3 (schemas + monitoring) → L4 (enforcement manifest + gates)
assessment_summary: 4/16 present, 7 partial, 5 absent. Strong operational tooling but no schema validation, no enforcement manifest, no versioning strategy.

choice: Current L2 (Structured) → Target L3 (Monitored)
rationale: 300+ scripts across the repo. Script ecosystem audit exists but infrastructure lacks formal schemas, consistent error handling, and monitoring. L3 establishes baseline.
effort: L (300+ scripts to audit and standardize)
assessment_summary: 3/16 present, 7 partial, 6 absent. Large volume, inconsistent patterns, some scripts unreachable. Ecosystem audit skill exists.

choice: Current L2 (Structured) → Target L3 (Monitored)
rationale: Documentation ecosystem has generation pipelines, index sync, templates. Needs monitoring for staleness, schema validation for doc metadata, and formalized contracts with other ecosystems.
assessment_summary: 3/16 present, 8 partial, 5 absent. Generation exists but sync checking is fragile. Doc-ecosystem-audit skill exists.

choice: Current L1 (Identified) → Target L3 (Monitored)
rationale: GitHub Actions workflows exist but lack formal ecosystem treatment. Pre-commit/pre-push hooks are in Hooks ecosystem. CI/CD covers the pipeline beyond local gates — build, deploy, integration checks.
assessment_summary: 1/16 present, 6 partial, 9 absent. Workflows exist but minimal formalization. Strong dependency on Hooks ecosystem.

choice: Current L1 (Identified) → Target L3 (Monitored)
rationale: Metrics, trend data, benchmarks scattered across health checkers and audit outputs. No unified analytics layer. L3 establishes schemas, monitoring, and consolidation.
staging: Staged: L1→L2 (identify + structure all metric sources) → L3 (monitoring + dashboards)
assessment_summary: 1/16 present, 5 partial, 10 absent. Metric data exists in health checkers and audits but no unified system. Cross-cuts many ecosystems.

choice: 5 ecosystems assessed: Alerts (L2→L4, M staged), Scripts (L2→L3, L), Docs (L2→L3, M), CI/CD (L1→L3, M staged), Analytics (L1→L3, M staged)
rationale: Middle-tier ecosystems. None at L0, none targeting L5. All need schema formalization and monitoring as baseline.

choice: Assessing: Planning/Roadmap, Testing, Archival/Rotation, and CANON (Ecosystem Zero)
rationale: Final ecosystem assessment batch. Includes CANON self-assessment which is unique — CANON assessing itself.

decision: Planning ecosystem renamed to Roadmap & Execution
choice: Rename 'Planning' → 'Roadmap & Execution'. Planning-as-skill stays in Skills ecosystem. Roadmap system, input pipelines, sprint execution = this ecosystem.
rationale: USER INSIGHT: Planning is a skill (deep-plan, GSD discovery). The SYSTEM is the roadmap — where work items land, get prioritized, get executed. Critical distinction. The ecosystem is the hub, not the skill. Multiple avenues must feed into it automatically (T8 automation over discipline).

in_scope: All input pipelines (debt, audits, deep-plan decisions, feature requests, architecture decisions)
out_of_scope_but_feeds_in: deep-plan skill (assessed in Skills ecosystem, feeds decisions INTO roadmap)
GSD discovery/planning agents (skill work, feeds INTO roadmap)
TDMS/MASTER_DEBT (assessed in TDMS ecosystem, feeds debt INTO roadmap)
Audit findings (assessed in respective audit ecosystems, feed INTO roadmap)

gap_identified: Non-debt input pipelines are MISSING. TDMS is the only semi-automated route. Audit findings, deep-plan decisions, feature planning, architecture decisions all require manual ROADMAP editing. T8 violation.

decision: Roadmap & Execution: Current and target maturity
choice: Current L2 (Structured) → Target L3 (Monitored)
rationale: L3 is correct target — establishes monitoring + input pipeline contracts. Hub ecosystem that everything feeds into. Non-debt input pipelines are the critical gap. Priority elevated due to hub nature — as each ecosystem is standardized, it generates work items that need automated intake.
effort: L (input pipeline contracts + sprint automation + ROADMAP schema)
staging: Direct (L2→L3). Focus on input pipeline automation.
assessment_summary: ROADMAP.md exists with sprint structure. GSD agents (11) operational. Sprint skill (6 phases) functional. task-next works. But: non-debt input pipelines missing, no schema validation on ROADMAP structure, no monitoring of pipeline health.
priority_reason: Hub ecosystem — every other ecosystem generates work items that need automated intake. Must be high in implementation order.
amendment_note: Removed hardcoded '13 ecosystems' per D90 (no hardcoded growing counts).

choice: Current L3 (Monitored) → Target L4 (Enforced)
rationale: Testing infrastructure has good coverage (500+ test files), pre-commit enforcement, CI integration. Delta to L4 is formalized enforcement manifest, Zod schemas for test config, and inter-ecosystem test contracts.
assessment_summary: 6/16 present, 6 partial, 4 absent. Strong operational testing but lacks formalized enforcement manifest and schema validation.

choice: Current L3 (Monitored) → Target L4 (Enforced)
rationale: Archival patterns exist (JSONL rotation, review archival, state cleanup). Monitoring via health checkers. Delta to L4 is enforcement gates, Zod schemas for archive format, and lifecycle hook formalization.
assessment_summary: 5/16 present, 6 partial, 5 absent. Rotation works operationally but lacks formal enforcement and schema validation.

decision: CANON (Ecosystem Zero): Self-assessment and enforcement model
choice: Current L0 (Nonexistent) → Target L5 (Canonized). STOUT enforcement system required — both internal (CANON self-protection) and downstream (cascade propagation to all 17 dependent ecosystems).
rationale: USER DIRECTIVE: L5+++ with emphasis on 'stout system.' CANON is the meta-system — everything builds from it. Enforcement must be bidirectional: (1) CANON changes are gated and validated, (2) downstream ecosystems are notified, migrated, and held to new standards. T1 (CANON is Ecosystem Zero), T8 (automation over discipline), T11 (fail loud fail early) all converge here.
effort: L (foundational but well-scoped — CANON defines itself first, D12/D32)
staging: Direct (L0→L5). First ecosystem standardized (D6). Self-dogfooding validates the framework.

internal_self_protection.pre_commit: Schema validation on .canon/ files, tenet changes require version bump, no orphaned references
internal_self_protection.pre_push: Migration script required for breaking changes, all tenet references valid across codebase
internal_self_protection.review_gate: CANON changes get elevated review (not any PR)
internal_self_protection.integrity_checks: tenets.jsonl ↔ tenets.md sync, ecosystems.jsonl ↔ per-ecosystem dirs sync

downstream_propagation.version_broadcast: CANON version bump → health checkers in all 17 ecosystems detect skew
downstream_propagation.contract_enforcement: Schema change → dependent enforcement manifests fail validation until updated
downstream_propagation.migration_automation: Breaking changes ship WITH migration scripts (T12 idempotent)
downstream_propagation.staggered_rollout: D19 pilot pattern — validate on 2-3 ecosystems before mass rollout
downstream_propagation.fail_loud_cascade: T11 — ecosystem >48h behind CANON version → alerts escalate to blocking

semver_blast_radius.minor: New checklist items/rules, maturity may shift, 24-48h auto PRs
semver_blast_radius.major: Breaking schema changes, all 17 must migrate, 1-2 weeks staggered

user_directive: There has got to be a stout system for canon changes both within the canon itself and downstream as well.
amendment_note: Ecosystem count updated from 13 to 17 to reflect D67 final count (18 total, 17 non-CANON dependents). Original count was from batch 5C before Agents, Audits, Frontend/App, Firebase/Backend were added in batch 5D.

choice: Current L2 (Structured) → Target L3 (Monitored)
rationale: 35 agent definitions with structured patterns, invocation JSONL tracking, but lacking formal health monitoring, Zod schemas, enforcement manifests. L3 adds monitoring without overcomplicating a functional system.
assessment_summary: Agent definitions exist with consistent patterns. Invocation tracking via JSONL. Missing: formal schemas, health checkers, enforcement gates, generated views.

choice: Current L3 (Monitored) → Target L4 (Enforced), with L5 pathway built in
rationale: 22 quality + 7 ecosystem audit skills, results in JSONL history, health scoring. Strong foundation. L4 adds enforcement gates. USER DIRECTIVE: pathway to L5 must be designed in from the start — not a future bolt-on.
assessment_summary: Mature ecosystem with JSONL storage, health scoring, templates. Missing: formal enforcement manifests, some schema validation, lifecycle hooks. L5 pathway = versioned audit standards, automated regression detection.
user_directive: L4 with a pathway to L5 built in somewhere for the near future

decision: Frontend/App: Current and target maturity + planning approach
choice: Current L2 (Structured) → Target L3 (Monitored). Adapted checklist (app-layer). Planning approach differs from process ecosystems — focus on connection points between process ecosystems and app layer.
rationale: Next.js 16, React 19, Tailwind 4 — 'part of but apart.' Has its own conventions but lacks CANON integration. Key insight: what matters most are the CONNECTIONS between process ecosystems and the app layer — points of contact, testing, and health monitoring at those boundaries. USER NOTE: Operational sprint already has health monitoring items that may overlap.
assessment_summary: Well-structured app layer with modern stack. Missing: CANON-aware health monitoring, formalized connection points to process ecosystems, schema validation at API boundaries.
user_insight: These last two ecosystems planning approach is going to be different. What will be important are the connections between process ecosystems and app ecosystems — those points of contact, testing, and health need to be solid.
user_note: Operational sprint had a number of health monitoring stuff in it

choice: Current L1 (Identified) → Target L3 (Monitored). Adapted checklist (app-layer). Staged approach given larger gap.
rationale: Cloud Functions, Firestore, Auth, Storage rules — functional but largely informal. Biggest maturity gap of the 18 ecosystems. Same app-layer planning approach as Frontend/App — connection points matter most.
assessment_summary: Firebase services functional but lack formalization. No schemas, no health monitoring, no enforcement. Firestore rules exist but aren't canonized. Cloud Functions lack lifecycle management.

choice: Formalized per-ecosystem required-vs-optional mapping. Rigid framework with built-in adaptability for different ecosystem types.
rationale: USER DIRECTIVE: 'Formalize it. Rigidity when necessary but ability to adapt to different situations while still maintaining order.' The 16-item CANON checklist doesn't apply uniformly — app-layer ecosystems (Frontend/App, Firebase/Backend) use Firestore not JSONL, have different view patterns, etc. Solution: formal mapping document per ecosystem declaring which items are required/optional/adapted, with justification. Order preserved; flexibility earned, not assumed.
user_directive: Formalize it. We want rigidity when necessary but the ability to adapt to different situations while still maintaining order.

implementation.enforcement: Mapping must exist before ecosystem plan begins — no ad-hoc exemptions
implementation.principles: Rigidity where needed (core items like health monitoring, testing), adaptability where warranted (storage format, view generation), order always maintained (formal justification required for any deviation)

choice: Two-doc system — DISCOVERY_RECORD.md (human-complete) + deep-plan.state.json (machine-complete). Both contain ALL decisions, tenets, directives, ideas, and assessments. Simultaneous-update rule: neither doc is allowed to lag the other.
rationale: Minimum viable separation. Human-readable narrative for audit/review/reference. Machine-readable JSON for session recovery and future automation. Two files, zero information asymmetry. Prefigures CANON's source-of-truth + generated-views pattern (T2). User: 'its basically one of the tenets anyway.'
enforcement: Simultaneous update — every decision, directive, idea, and tenet MUST exist in BOTH files at the same time. No lag allowed.

documents.human: DISCOVERY_RECORD.md — narrative record with all decisions, rationale, user context, tenets, directives, ideas, assessment tables, version history
documents.machine: deep-plan.state.json — structured JSON with same data + resume instructions, batch status, timestamps, implementation metadata

supersession_note: Two-doc system evolved into JSONL-source + generated-MD-views architecture. state.json decomposed into 5 JSONL files (D77). DISCOVERY_RECORD.md became a scripted generated view from JSONL (D79). Simultaneous-update enforcement no longer applies.

choice: Tiered waves (4-5 waves of 3-5 ecosystems each) with checkpoint validation between waves
rationale: T10 (validate before scaling) + T13 (discoveries feed forward). Parallelism within waves, learning transfer between them.
supersession_note: Wave model replaced by sequential-first execution model (D63). Checkpoint validation concept preserved in D67 checkpoints.

choice: CANON solo — Wave 1 is exclusively CANON (L0→L5)
rationale: Ecosystem Zero (T1) needs undivided attention. Self-dogfoods the framework. All subsequent waves depend on CANON contracts.
supersession_note: CANON remains position #1 in D67 sequence. Wave framing replaced by sequential model.

choice: PR Review goes in Wave 2 as explicit CANON pilot (S effort, L4→L5)
rationale: Smallest delta, proves CANON framework works on a real ecosystem before scaling. T10 validated. USER QUESTION: entire operation mapped in advance with stages scheduled — YES, wave assignments mapped now, each ecosystem deep-planned JIT per T13.
supersession_note: PR Review at position #4 in D67 sequence. Pilot role preserved.

choice: TDMS L2→L3 starts in Wave 2, L3→L4 in Wave 3-4, L5 in Wave 5. Staged across full timeline.
rationale: Too critical to defer (user directive: feeds into everything), but XL scope requires staging across waves.
supersession_note: TDMS staged at positions #8 (L2→L3), #16 (L3→L4), #21 (L4→L5) in D67. Three-stage approach preserved.

choice: Hooks + Testing in Wave 2 for enforcement gates. Skills ELEVATED to Wave 2 (user directive: main tool, touches many systems).
rationale: Enforcement infra early so all subsequent waves have formal gates. Skills elevated by user — it's a primary daily tool with cross-ecosystem dependencies.
supersession_note: Skills elevated to #2, Hooks at #3, Testing at #6 in D67. Elevation intent fully preserved.

choice: Frontend/App + Firebase/Backend in the final wave (last)
rationale: User insight: connection points between app and process layers are what matters. Standardize process ecosystems first so connections are well-defined.
supersession_note: Frontend #18, Firebase #19 in D67. Last-position intent preserved.

decision: Parallelism within waves — SUPERSEDED by D63
choice: SUPERSEDED. Originally: 2-3 parallel max within waves. Replaced by sequential-first approach (D63) after deep research revealed cross-ecosystem cascade makes true parallelism unreliable.
rationale: Deep research across 13 agents + user analysis revealed: every ecosystem overhaul creates/modifies skills, hooks, scripts, and docs. The 'independent tracks' assumption breaks down when considering outputs of standardization, not just inputs. Superseded by D63.

choice: Sequential implementation with research overlap. One ecosystem at a time. Research/deep-plan ecosystem N+1 while implementing ecosystem N. No parallel implementation tracks.
rationale: USER INSIGHT + deep research synthesis: (1) Every ecosystem overhaul creates/modifies skills, hooks, scripts, docs — so parallel implementation causes cross-contamination. (2) Learning transfer (T13) is the whole point — parallel prevents it. (3) Changelog tracking (T18) is clean and linear when sequential. (4) User's attention is focused, not fragmented. (5) Each ecosystem benefits from ALL previous ones. Research overlap preserves productivity without sacrificing learning.
research_overlap: Research/deep-plan for ecosystem N+1 runs in parallel with implementation of ecosystem N. This is the ONLY parallelism approved.

choice: Every ecosystem change that affects another system MUST be logged in a standardized JSONL changelog (.canon/changelog.jsonl). Cross-ecosystem impact tracked at every step. Tenet of at least this system overhaul, may be promoted to permanent.
rationale: USER DIRECTIVE: 'changelogs need to be standardized and it needs to be a tenet of at least this system overhaul.' Overlap between ecosystems is inevitable — change one thing, have to change another. Changelog becomes the connective tissue. Aligns with T14 (capture everything) but specific to cross-ecosystem traceability.

schema_sketch.format: JSONL — one line per cross-ecosystem change

decision: Docs elevated to position #5 in sequence
choice: Docs ecosystem standardized at position #5 (after CANON, Skills, Hooks, PR Review pilot). Establishes folder structure, file placement standards, document dependency maps, master lists BEFORE any further ecosystems are standardized.
rationale: USER DIRECTIVE: 'I think I want docs at number five.' Too much content in docs (100+ files) to defer. Every subsequent ecosystem produces docs — if doc standards aren't established early, 13+ ecosystems produce non-compliant docs that need rework. Docs at #5 means ecosystems #6-#18 create born-compliant documentation. Docs verification pass at #20 confirms consistency.
scope_at_position_5: Folder structure and file placement standards
Document dependency maps (which docs reference which)
Master lists and index sync patterns
Documentation templates for all subsequent ecosystems
verification_pass: Position #20 — lightweight meta-check after all ecosystems complete

decision: CI/CD restored to sequence at position #10
choice: CI/CD (L1→L3, M staged) restored to the sequence at position #10, between Scripts (#9) and Alerts (#11).
rationale: Was accidentally dropped from the initial sequential proposal. CI/CD (D41: GitHub Actions workflows, build/deploy pipelines, integration checks) benefits from Hooks (#3) and Testing (#6) being standardized, and from Scripts (#9) establishing script standards.

choice: Sequential execution order locked. 18 unique ecosystems. TDMS staged at #8, #16, #21. Docs at #5 + verification at #20. Research overlap is the only approved parallelism.
rationale: Synthesizes all Batch 6 decisions (D56-D66) plus deep research from 13 agents covering all 18 ecosystems. Maximizes T13 learning transfer, enables T18 changelog tracking, gets tools (Skills, Hooks) and standards (Docs) online early.

sequence.rationale: Foundation. Tenets, schemas, changelog mechanism (T18).
Daily tool. Skill-audit canonized. Born-compliant skills for all future work.
Enforcement infra. Skill validation gates formalized.
CANON pilot. CHECKPOINT: Does framework work?
Folder structure, placement standards, dependency maps, master lists. Born-compliant docs for all future ecosystems.
Test infrastructure. Complements hooks. Proper test patterns for all subsequent work.
State management. Cross-cutting infra. Now with skills, hooks, testing, docs to enforce it.
Critical system, first stage. 37 scripts get Zod schemas + monitoring.
Script infrastructure standards. Informed by TDMS learnings.
Build/deploy pipelines. Benefits from hooks and testing being standard.
Monitoring layer. Benefits from all infra being standardized.
Aggregation layer. Builds on alerts + health checker patterns.
Agent definitions. Benefits from Skills and Hooks.
All audits are skills. L4 with L5 pathway. Benefits from Skills standardization.
Lifecycle patterns. Benefits from JSONL and schema standards.
Second stage. Enforcement manifest, testing.
Hub ecosystem. All input pipelines now standardized. Automated intake.
App-layer. All process-side connections defined.
App-layer. Connection point contracts.
Meta-check: all 18 ecosystems docs consistent? Staleness audit.
Final canonization of the critical system.

checkpoints.gate: Does CANON framework work? PR Review pilot validates end-to-end.
Core infrastructure complete. Skills, Hooks, Docs, Testing, Sessions all standardized. Ready for data-heavy ecosystems.
All process-layer ecosystems standardized. Ready for app-layer.
Full system overhaul complete. End-of-process audit.

choice: Option B: Skip & Return. If ecosystem N stalls, move to N+1, mark N as 'deferred with context.' Changelog documents what downstream ecosystems will miss and what may need revisiting when N completes.
rationale: Sequential model shouldn't become a bottleneck. T18 changelog captures the gap naturally. Stalls happen.

choice: Option B+C: All checklist items addressed (completed or formally deferred with justification per D54) PLUS brief user review. Scorecard + deferral justifications presented for approval before proceeding.
rationale: Combines measurability (checklist) with interactivity (T15). Formal deferral prevents silent skipping. User stays in the loop without heavy overhead.

choice: Option D: Hybrid — Track-CANON for the overhaul's 21-step sequence as big picture, individual ecosystem work items in their natural tracks, cross-references. AMENDMENT: Each time items are added to ROADMAP (in groupings), run a dedupe against MASTER_DEBT to prevent duplication.
rationale: Best visibility. Track-CANON shows the big picture, existing tracks show the work. Tests Roadmap & Execution ecosystem before standardizing it at #17. Dedupe prevents ROADMAP additions from creating duplicate MASTER_DEBT entries.
user_amendment: run a dedupe from the roadmap and master debt each time items are added in groupings

choice: Option B: Interleaved. Standardization and project work in same sessions as needed. Soft guideline: if 3+ sessions pass with no standardization progress, ecosystem health dashboard flags it.
rationale: Natural workflow. Deep-plan state files handle pause/resume. T8 automation over discipline — system nudges, not willpower.

choice: Option C: Extensible core. Required: {timestamp, ecosystem, change, affects}. Optional: {type, seq_position, decision_ref, files_changed, severity}. Schema lives in .canon/schemas/changelog.schema.json (Zod source of truth per D24). Mirrors D22 pattern.
rationale: Same extensible-core pattern used for ecosystem registry. Avoids schema bloat for simple changes, allows rich context for complex ones.

choice: Option B: Full reassessment at TDMS L2->L3 (#8) with lightweight pre-checks before each ecosystem (#1-#7). Added as explicit task in the plan.
rationale: Informed reassessment — changelog shows exactly what changed across ecosystems #1-#7. Pre-checks catch obvious invalidations early.
task_description: Before each ecosystem #1-#7: scan changelog for MASTER_DEBT items referencing files about to be modified, flag for review. At #8: full Grand Plan reassessment.

choice: Option B: Per-ecosystem. CANON (#1) defines the contract FORMAT (structure, location, required fields). Each ecosystem defines its actual contracts during its own deep-plan, with already-standardized ecosystems as partners.
rationale: Contracts grow organically and are concrete. Ecosystem #14 has 13 potential contract partners. Changelog shows how they interact.

decision: Framework repo and knowledge base integration
choice: Option B: Per-ecosystem consultation. Each ecosystem's Phase 0 includes checking framework repo for relevant decisions/patterns. EXPANDED: All prior research forms a knowledge base — framework repo (68 decisions, 42 gaps), current standards and practices, PR Review v2 patterns, prior research agents' findings, all decision logs from this deep-plan. This knowledge base is the starting point for every ecosystem deep-plan.
rationale: USER INSIGHT: current standards, PR review patterns, prior research, and decision logs all form a database to pull from when designing any ecosystem. Excellent starting point for each deep-plan Phase 0.
knowledge_base_sources: Current standards and practices (CLAUDE.md, CODE_PATTERNS.md, SKILL_STANDARDS.md)
PR Review v2 as reference implementation (Zod schemas, health checkers, enforcement manifest)
Deep-research agent findings from Batch 6 (all ecosystems mapped)
This deep-plan: all decisions, tenets, and user directives
amendment_note: Removed hardcoded counts per D90 (no hardcoded growing counts). Was: 68 decisions, 42 gaps, 5 Zod schemas, 10 health checkers, 13 agents, 76+ decisions, 18 tenets, 22+ directives.

decision: CANON versioning and learning capture during overhaul
choice: Option B (version at checkpoints) with critical amendment: learning capture happens DURING ecosystem builds, not just between them. Any option that delays learning capture to checkpoint boundaries is a non-starter. Version bumps at checkpoints (0.1.0 -> 1.0.0 at checkpoint #1 -> 1.1.0 -> 1.2.0 -> 1.3.0). Patch escape valve for urgent CANON updates.
rationale: USER DIRECTIVE: 'learnings need to not only be caught ecosystem to ecosystem but during ecosystem builds. any option that doesn't allow for that is a non-starter.' Versioning cadence is secondary — the learning capture mechanism is primary. Changelog (T18) is the continuous capture mechanism. CANON versions are the batched formalization.

learning_capture_mechanism.during_ecosystem: Changelog JSONL captures every cross-ecosystem impact in real time. Learnings logged as they happen.
learning_capture_mechanism.between_ecosystems: T13 plan-as-you-go — next ecosystem's deep-plan reads changelog + previous ecosystem's outcomes
learning_capture_mechanism.at_checkpoints: CANON version bump formalizes accumulated learnings into updated schemas, tenets, or checklist items
learning_capture_mechanism.emergency: Patch version for urgent CANON updates that can't wait for checkpoint

choice: Option B with path to C: Decompose state.json into purpose-specific JSONL files (decisions.jsonl, tenets.jsonl, directives.jsonl, ideas.jsonl, changelog.jsonl) + coordination.json. MD views generated from JSONL. When CANON is built (#1), tenets.jsonl migrates to .canon/ and planning artifacts become first CANON test case.
rationale: Follows T4 (JSONL-first) and T2 (source of truth + generated views). Each artifact independently queryable. Smaller files = less compaction risk. Current state.json at ~1000 lines is becoming unwieldy.

files: tenets.jsonl — one line per tenet (T1-T18+), later migrates to .canon/
directives.jsonl — one line per user directive
ideas.jsonl — one line per captured idea
coordination.json — batch status, resume instructions, phase tracking

decision: Safety and redundancy for planning artifacts
choice: Option B+C: Git commits at every batch (primary). Tagged commits at 4 checkpoints (canon-checkpoint-1 through canon-checkpoint-4). MCP episodic memory summary at each checkpoint for cross-session discovery.
rationale: Multiple independent copies. Tagged commits are hard to lose. MCP memory enables cross-session search. JSONL files are source of truth — everything else is backup.

decision: Format optimization — JSONL source + generated MD views
choice: Option A: JSONL files are canonical source (AI-optimized). MD files are generated views (human-optimized). Generation is SCRIPTED — Node.js script reads JSONL → produces DISCOVERY_RECORD.md. Script runs after every batch as a gate. Script becomes template for all future ecosystem view generators.
rationale: T2 applied to our own planning process. Eliminates the dual-maintenance problem. DISCOVERY_RECORD.md is currently behind (needs D55-D76) — under this system, it can never fall behind because it's generated.

process_tree.step_1: User says something → AI classifies as decision/directive/idea/tenet/correction/question
process_tree.step_2: Immediate JSONL append to the right file (real-time during conversation)
process_tree.step_3: Batch gate (end of Q&A batch): update coordination.json, run generate-discovery.js, git commit
process_tree.step_4: Safety: git commit every batch, tagged commits at checkpoints, MCP at milestones

script: scripts/planning/generate-discovery-record.js — reads all JSONL → produces DISCOVERY_RECORD.md. Template for all future view generators.
user_question_answered: Yes, generation is scripted. The gate is: end of Q&A batch triggers generation + commit.

decision: Standard for future deep-plans — always full suite
choice: REVISED to always full suite. All ecosystem deep-plans use the complete JSONL artifact structure. No lightweight tier. Consistency across 18 ecosystems is worth more than saving files for smaller ones.
rationale: USER DIRECTIVE: lean heavy-handed. Deep-plan floor is ~15 questions, most ecosystems will exceed that. Full suite means every ecosystem's artifacts are queryable in the same way, feeding the knowledge base (D75).

choice: 26 audit domains across 4 tiers. Tier 1: Core (5 mechanical checks). Tier 2: Analytical (7 deep analysis). Tier 3: Implementation (10 build-time checks with phase-level + full-scope split). Tier 4: Ecosystem Completion (4 exit checks). Reusable across all audit contexts.
rationale: USER DIRECTIVE: thorough audits at all levels. Gap analysis + re-research with fresh eyes. Implementation audits are the missing layer — auditing the BUILD itself, not just the plan or final state. Phase-level audits catch drift early; full-scope confirms everything at the end.

tier_1_core: 1. Decision Coverage — every ecosystem assessed, no unresolved questions
2. Tenet Alignment — decisions traceable to tenets, no orphans
3. Directive Compliance — all user directives addressed
4. Sequence Integrity — no dependency gaps, checkpoints defined
5. Artifact Completeness — all JSONL, MD, state files exist
tier_2_analytical: 6. Gap Analysis — plan vs original diagnosis, emerged vs planned, decision-to-delivery gaps
7. Re-Research with Fresh Eyes — agents re-examine codebase through lens of completed plan, compare against original findings
8. Contradiction Detection — systematic scan for conflicting decisions
9. Risk Assessment — per-ecosystem completion risks, dependencies, scope explosion potential
10. Cross-Ecosystem Impact Projection — pre-populate expected downstream effects per ecosystem
11. Knowledge Base Readiness — is the knowledge base (D75) actually ready for ecosystem #1?
12. Rollback Viability — what's the plan if checkpoint #1 reveals CANON is flawed?

tier_3_implementation.description: Audits the BUILD itself. Two levels: phase-level (lightweight, frequent) and full-scope (comprehensive, once per ecosystem).
phase_level_domains: I1. Plan Adherence — implementation following the deep-plan? Deviations documented?
I2. Changelog Discipline — every cross-ecosystem change logged per T18?
I3. Code Quality — code-reviewer pass, CLAUDE.md patterns, security checklist
I4. Born-Compliant — new artifacts (skills, hooks, scripts, docs) comply with previously standardized ecosystems?
I6. Learning Capture — learnings captured DURING build per D76? Insights in changelog?
I7. Scope Containment — staying within ecosystem boundary? No creep into later ecosystems?
I8. Test Coverage — new patterns tested? Health checkers pass? Enforcement gates enforcing?
I10. Documentation Sync — docs updated as implementation proceeds?
full_scope_only_domains: I5. Regression Detection — health checkers for ALL completed ecosystems, not just current
I9. User Directive Satisfaction — ecosystem-specific user directives honored?

tier_4_ecosystem_completion: 13. Completeness Scorecard — 16-item CANON checklist: completed vs formally deferred with justification
14. Integration Verification — new contracts work with previously standardized ecosystems
15. Changelog Accuracy — changelog vs git diff cross-reference
16. Learning Harvest — what feeds into next ecosystem, what triggers CANON patch

implementation_audit_flow: Phase 1 build → Phase 1 audit (7 domains) → Phase 2 build → Phase 2 audit (7 domains) → ... → Final phase → Full-scope audit (14 domains: I1-I10 + Tier 4)

choice: Interactive, conversational audit at all levels. AI produces analysis, presents in batches, user reacts, iterate until satisfied. Operates as we've been operating throughout this deep-plan. Not a batch script dump. Applies to phase audits (lighter touch) and full-scope audits (deeper discussion).
rationale: USER DIRECTIVE: 'interactive as all skill functions should be even if it isn't an actual skill. operate as we've been operating.' Aligns with T15 (interactivity-first).

choice: Plan audits: twice (after DECISIONS.md and after PLAN.md). Implementation audits: at every phase boundary (lightweight) + full-scope at ecosystem completion. Overhaul audit: at checkpoint #21.
rationale: Smaller phase audits catch drift before it compounds. Full-scope confirms everything. Two plan audits catch decision issues before they become plan issues.

choice: When any decision is superseded (fully or partially), the superseding decision MUST add superseded_by, status, and supersession_note fields to the original. This is enforced at batch gate time.
rationale: Phase 1b audit found 7 decisions (D55-D61) missing supersession markers, causing readers to get wrong execution model. D62 had the right pattern but it was ad-hoc. This formalizes it.

required_fields.supersession_note: Brief explanation of what changed and what survived

protocol.timing: At the same time the new decision is recorded — not retroactively
protocol.responsibility: The session recording the superseding decision adds markers to the original
protocol.enforcement: Batch gate script should warn if a decision references supersedes without corresponding superseded_by on the target

decision: Decision amendment protocol + JSONL→MD sync enforcement
choice: Two-tier change model: (1) Amendments for corrections (stale counts, typos, factual updates) — add amended_in field + changelog entry, edit in-place. (2) Supersession for replacements — use D84 protocol. JSONL→MD sync enforced via pre-commit hook.
rationale: Phase 1b audit found stale counts (D49: 13→17, D32: T16→T18, D81: 7→8) that need correction without full supersession. Also found no mechanism to validate JSONL source matches generated MD views.

amendment_protocol.when: Factual correction, stale count, typo, or non-substantive update

required_fields.amended_in: batch or audit ID that made the change

amendment_protocol.changelog: Append entry to changelog.jsonl with type 'amendment'

sync_enforcement.action: Regenerates MD via dry-run, compares to committed MD, blocks if stale

triggered_by: Phase 1b audit finding S0-2 + user request

decision: Migration path mechanics — deferred to PLAN.md
choice: Migration script conventions, data transformation tooling, migration testing, and rollback behavior will be defined during PLAN.md phase when concrete schema changes are known. D49's 'breaking changes ship WITH migration scripts' principle stands; PLAN.md must specify the mechanics.
rationale: Phase 1b audit found D49 states the principle but not the implementation. Defining conventions now without concrete schemas would be premature. PLAN.md will have the context of actual TDMS (37 scripts) and Skills (65 skills) migration needs.
plan_md_requirement: PLAN.md MUST include a 'Migration Mechanics' section defining: naming conventions, file locations, up/down function format, dry-run testing, and rollback behavior.

choice: S=1-2 sessions, M=3-5 sessions, L=6-10 sessions, XL=11-20 sessions. XL-partial (staged) ecosystems like TDMS use per-stage sizing. These are planning estimates, not commitments.
rationale: Phase 1b audit found effort labels (S/M/L/XL) used across 21-step sequence with no concrete calibration. Without session-count ranges, no way to ballpark total overhaul scope or identify resource bottlenecks.

calibration.S: 1-2 sessions — small delta, well-understood scope (e.g., PR Review L4→L5)
calibration.M: 3-5 sessions — moderate scope, some discovery needed (e.g., Hooks L3→L4, Testing L3→L4)
calibration.L: 6-10 sessions — large scope, significant restructuring (e.g., CANON L0→L5, Skills L1→L3, Scripts L2→L3)
calibration.XL: 11-20 sessions — massive scope, multiple phases (e.g., TDMS full L2→L5 across 3 stages)

projection.note: Refinable during PLAN.md when task breakdowns exist

decision: Tenet alignment soft reminder at batch gate + backfill script
choice: Batch gate warns (non-blocking) when new decisions have no tenet reference anywhere in their fields. backfill-tenet-evidence.js runs at every batch gate to maintain bidirectional tenet↔decision links. Gaps accepted for procedural decisions.
rationale: Phase 1b audit found 59/83 decisions had no reverse link from tenets. Backfill script added 27 evidence links. Soft reminder ensures future decisions consider tenet alignment without over-constraining procedural entries.

enforcement.batch_gate: Warn if new decision has no T{n} reference in any field
enforcement.reason: Some decisions (D84 supersession protocol, D83 audit triggers) are legitimately procedural and don't map to tenets

triggered_by: Phase 1b audit finding S1-4 + user question about creation-time enforcement

decision: Dependency graph for 21-step sequence — deferred to PLAN.md
choice: Formal dependency map deferred to PLAN.md phase. D67 rationale text contains implicit dependencies but formalizing them requires task-level decomposition that only PLAN.md can provide.
rationale: Phase 1b audit found D67 has no formal dependency graph. Creating one now without task-level granularity would be premature. PLAN.md will decompose each step into tasks with explicit dependency declarations.
plan_md_requirement: PLAN.md MUST include a dependency graph showing: (1) which ecosystems depend on which others, (2) critical path identification, (3) validation of D68's skip-and-return mechanism against actual dependencies.

decision: No hardcoded counts of growing collections — repo-wide
choice: Never hardcode a count of any collection that grows over time (ecosystems, tenets, decisions, skills, scripts, etc.) unless a maintenance procedure ensures the count stays accurate. Use dynamic references ('current ecosystems'), approximate markers ('70+'), or computed values instead. Applies repo-wide — not just planning artifacts.
rationale: USER DIRECTIVE: 'hardcoding any number of anything that will change over time isn't a good idea unless a maintenance procedure is in place' and 'this should be same in any file throughout the repo.' Phase 1b audit found stale counts in D31 (16→18 tenets), D46 (13→18 ecosystems), D49 (13→17 ecosystems, already fixed), D67 title (18 ecosystems, 21 steps).
enforcement: Code review flag. Future: lint rule that detects numeric literals near collection keywords (ecosystems, tenets, skills, scripts) without a + suffix or dynamic reference.
triggered_by: Phase 1b audit finding S2-1 + user directive
